news.title: FONRWA seeks action against unauthorised PGs in Noida
news.para: Noida residents' welfare association seeks action against unauthorized paying guest accommodations.  These PGs reportedly lack essential fire safety equipment and pose risks.  Residents cite parking scarcity and sanitation issues caused by these operations.  The association has repeatedly flagged concerns to the Noida Authority.  They demand a survey and closure of illegal facilities to ensure safety.

news.title: UltraTech Cement’s net profit rises 17% to ₹2,603.72 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: UltraTech Cement’s consolidated total income increased 15.5 per cent to ₹24,778.47 crore during the quarter. Its domestic grey cement capacity crossed 200.1 million tonnes per annum.

news.title: Goa plans to convert industrial land from lease to freehold
news.para: Goa Industrial Development Corporation is working on reforms for industrial land ownership conversion.  The corporation is also considering easing rules for plot transfers and subdivisions.  These changes align with the Centre's deregulation initiative for industrial land use.  Allottees will pay a freehold premium to extinguish reversionary rights under the new framework.  Proceeds will fund a corpus, with only interest available for operational expenditure.

news.title: Chennai civic body collects ₹103 crore property tax in 14 days
news.para: Chennai Corporation collected over ₹103 crore in property tax dues in just fourteen days. This sharp rise resulted from a special intensive collection drive held across the city. Property owners voluntarily came forward to clear their tax dues during this period. Officials are continuing the drive to recover long-pending dues from remaining owners. Payments can be made through various convenient channels for property owners.

news.title: Submit DLF City demolition and restoration records: HC to Haryana
news.para: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has ordered the state government to submit demolition and restoration details.  This action concerns properties allegedly involved in unauthorized constructions within DLF City.  The court seeks building bylaws to verify compliance with planning regulations.  It aims to protect lawful buildings while addressing violations.  The court also requires property owners to furnish relevant documentation for adjudication.

news.title: Telangana government to launch one lakh Indiramma houses for the poor in CURE on July 20
news.para: The Indiramma housing scheme for low-income groups will launch Monday at the secretariat.  The government plans to construct one lakh multi-storey flats within the core urban region.  Application processes and eligibility criteria will be released by ministers at the event.  This initiative focuses on prime land parcels close to existing poor communities.  Construction of 3.50 lakh houses across rural and urban areas is planned this fiscal.

news.title: Consumer court holds Bank of Baroda liable for missed PMAY benefit
news.para: A consumer commission has ordered Bank of Baroda to pay Rs 3.75 lakh to a home loan borrower. The bank failed to process the borrower's application for a Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana subsidy. This failure resulted in the borrower being deprived of a Rs 2.35 lakh subsidy. Additionally, the borrower paid Rs 1.4 lakh in extra interest on his home loan.

news.title: Punjab simplifies property registration language, replaces 35 complex revenue terms
news.para: Punjab has replaced thirty-five complex revenue terms with simpler Punjabi words. This initiative aims to make property registration documents more accessible to citizens. The government approved this move under Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh Mann's leadership. Officials have been instructed to implement the revised terminology immediately. This change enhances transparency and citizen-friendliness in government services.

news.title: Protest in Thane against Amazon data centre; company says it won't divert local water, electricity
news.para: Thane residents are protesting Amazon's proposed data centre due to environmental worries.  Protesters claim the facility will cause pollution and consume significant resources.  Amazon states it complies with environmental laws and uses dedicated power sources.  The company also asserts it does not use local water for cooling purposes.  Local leaders have requested a review of project permissions and public hearings.

news.title: Chhattisgarh EOW files chargesheet in 2,000 loan case; loss rises from ₹1.86 crore to ₹104 crore
news.para: Chhattisgarh EOW filed a chargesheet in a 26-year-old loan fraud case.  Fraudulent loans worth Rs 1.86 crore were obtained between 1995 and 1998.  These loans have now grown into a bad debt of about Rs 104 crore.  Three former officials and a society president face charges for misappropriating funds.  The accused allegedly prepared forged documents for fake beneficiaries and obtained loans.

news.title: Gurugram: Residents protest hike in upkeep fee at Godrej Oasis
news.para: Godrej Oasis residents protested a significant increase in common area maintenance charges. They cited poor services and a lack of financial transparency from the developer. Housekeeping staff stopped work over unpaid salaries, highlighting the deteriorating situation. Residents demanded a rollback of the hike and improved society maintenance. They also raised concerns about the poor quality of borewell water supplied.

news.title: Jaipur development body razes two illegal colonies in Kanota
news.para: According to JDA enforcement wing deputy inspector general of police Anand Sharma, one colony was being developed on around two bighas in Kanota village on Dudhawala Road near Anand College

news.title: Jaipur development body's setback rule slows residential-to-commercial conversions
news.para: Jaipur Development Authority's new rule mandates setback violation rectification before conversions.  More than eighty percent of applications remain pending due to these violations.  Most applications were rejected as owners showed no interest in rectifying issues.  Previously, conditional approvals were granted, but this practice has now stopped.  Applicants argue that rectifying violations is premature before other necessary clearances.

news.title: Hyderabad: HMDA seeks de-notification of Kokapet IT SEZ to unlock high-value land
news.para: Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority plans to auction prime Kokapet land.  This move follows the failure of a nearly two-decade-old IT SEZ project.  Legal disputes and non-payment prevented the special economic zone from becoming operational.  The authority expects to generate approximately Rs 12,000 crore from the land auction.  De-notification from the Centre is required before the auction process can begin.

news.title: JK Cement's profit down 15.3% to ₹274.62 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: JK Cement reported a 15.3 percent profit decline for the June quarter.  The company's net profit fell to Rs 274.62 crore from Rs 324.25 crore.  Revenue from operations increased by 20.25 percent to Rs 4,031.72 crore.  Total expenses for the quarter rose by 25.5 percent.  JK Cement's total income, including other income, grew 19.41 percent.

news.title: Karnataka government forms panel to fix e-Swathu bottlenecks, explores B-Khata for rural properties
news.para: A five-member committee will resolve e-Swathu record issues for rural property owners. Technical and server problems have caused many applications to remain pending. The committee will identify bottlenecks and suggest rule amendments for doorstep delivery. It will also examine issuing certificates to long-term Gramathana residents. The government is considering B-Khata for properties outside Gramathana limits.

news.title: ED conducts raids in Rajasthan, Delhi & Bihar over ₹131-crore illegal sand mining case
news.para: Enforcement Directorate conducted searches across Bihar, Delhi, and Rajasthan.  These actions targeted a company allegedly involved in illegal sand mining.  The racket is estimated to be worth over ₹131 crore in Bihar's Banka district.  Geospatial analysis confirmed extensive illegal excavation between 2015 and 2023.  The investigation focuses on laundering proceeds from this alleged illegal activity.
